dimension_notes.dependency_health: pom.xml is clean and well-organised: Jackson BOM used for consistent versioning, jackson-core pinned with documented rationale, SLF4J+Logback+log4j-to-slf4j is a standard chain for a project depending on BlackLab. No duplicate libraries for the same purpose. Minor long-term risk from deliberate use of com.sun.net.httpserver (jdk.httpserver public API) across 6+ source files; a tracked TODO already notes migration path. The non-Maven-Central BlackLab 4.0.0 dependency requiring local install is clearly documented with a CI enforcement note and a labelled fallback profile.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Most code is clean and direct with good use of Java 21 records, sealed interfaces, and explicit guard clauses. Issues: (1) anonymous-subclass hack in BlackLabQueryExecutor test constructor; (2) stale section-break migration comment in CqlUtils; (3) executeRelationQueries carries 6 parameters with a BiFunction callback, blurring intent.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: Boundaries are generally well-designed: SketchResponseAssembler and ExploreResponseAssembler own model-to-presentation translation, SharedExploreParams and ExamplesContext carry boundary data cleanly. Main issue: ExplorationResult is a dual-mode container (single-seed vs multi-seed) whose seed() method returns a comma-joined string in multi-seed mode. Assembler callers must know which mode was used; nothing in the type enforces this. The seam works today but is fragile by convention rather than contract.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: No restating comments or docstring bloat on trivial code. The class-level Javadocs are thorough and add real value. Main issue: parameter-echo debug logging in CollocateProfileComparator (lines 62-63) and SingleSeedExplorer (line 81) logs all input parameters at method entry and prints summary at method exit — a hallmark AI trace-logging pattern that adds execution noise without explaining why. The guards (isDebugEnabled()) are in place but the signals carry no information beyond what a debugger already shows.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: No coexisting old+new API patterns or deprecated-but-called symbols. A stale migration comment in CqlUtils.java (line 167) left from when methods were moved from a now-deleted BcqlPatternUtils class. Stale 'formerly word1 / formerly word2' history in ConcordanceHandlers Javadoc. These are residue documentation artifacts, not live migration debt.

issues.summary: Unnecessary empty anonymous subclass created in BlackLabQueryExecutor test constructor
evidence: Line 70: `this.collocateQueryHelper = index != null ? new CollocateQueryHelper(index) : new CollocateQueryHelper((BlackLabIndex) null) {};`

The trailing `{}` creates an anonymous subclass with an empty body, which is functionally identical to `new CollocateQueryHelper(null)` but semantically confusing — it implies a behavioural override that does not exist.
issues.suggestion: Replace `new CollocateQueryHelper((BlackLabIndex) null) {}` with `new CollocateQueryHelper((BlackLabIndex) null)` (plain instantiation). If the intent is to allow test subclasses that override I/O methods, document that via a protected constructor on CollocateQueryHelper rather than an anonymous class at the call site.

issues.summary: Stale migration comment divides CqlUtils into sections after the referenced class no longer exists
evidence: Line 167: `// BCQL pattern utilities (moved from BcqlPatternUtils in query package)`
BcqlPatternUtils no longer exists; the migration is complete. The comment now creates a false impression of a structural division inside a single utility class.
issues.suggestion: Remove the `// BCQL pattern utilities (moved from BcqlPatternUtils in query package)` comment. If a logical grouping is still desired, use a blank line separator without the historical note.

issues.summary: executeRelationQueries has 6 parameters including a complex BiFunction callback, obscuring intent
evidence: Method signature: `executeRelationQueries(RelationType, Predicate<RelationConfig>, String, Map<String,RelationEntry>, List<String>, BiFunction<RelationConfig,ExecutedSketch,RelationEntry>)`

Callers pass inline lambdas that call either `buildSurfaceRelationEntry` or `buildDepRelationEntry`, which are single-method calls. The abstraction of a shared loop with a builder callback hides that the two callers differ only in the assembler method called.
issues.suggestion: Extract the two call sites into focused private methods (`executeAndCollectSurfaceRelations`, `executeAndCollectDepRelations`), each with the RelationEntry builder inlined. This removes the BiFunction parameter and makes both call paths self-documenting without sharing a six-parameter method.

issues.summary: ExplorationResult.seed() returns a comma-joined multi-seed string, making single-seed assembly fragile by convention
evidence: ExplorationResult.seed() returns `String.join(",", seeds)` — correct for single-seed display but produces a comma-joined string like 'theory,model,hypothesis' in multi-seed mode.

ExploreResponseAssembler.buildSingleSeedExploreResponse passes `result.seed()` as the `seed` field of SingleSeed response; this only works correctly because callers guarantee single-seed mode. Nothing in the type system enforces this.
If buildSingleSeedExploreResponse were accidentally called with a multi-seed ExplorationResult, it would silently produce a malformed JSON `seed` field containing a comma-joined string.
issues.suggestion: Add a `assertSingleSeed()` guard or replace `result.seed()` with `result.seeds().get(0)` in `buildSingleSeedExploreResponse` to make the single-seed assumption explicit and detectable at runtime. Alternatively, have the factory method `ExplorationResult.of(List<String> seeds, ...)` validate that single-seed responses only carry one seed if the assembler path enforces it.

issues.summary: Method-entry parameter echo logging and method-exit summary logging in exploration classes with no diagnostic value beyond a debugger
evidence: CollocateProfileComparator.compareCollocateProfiles lines 62-63: `logger.debug("Nouns: {}", seedNouns); logger.debug("Min logDice: {}", minLogDice);` — echoes the method's two parameters verbatim.
SingleSeedExplorer.explore line 81: `logger.debug("Exploring semantic field: seed='{}', relation='{}', top={}, minShared={}, minLogDice={}", ...)` — echoes all five input parameters.
SingleSeedExplorer.explore line 108: `logger.debug("Exploration complete for '{}': {} nouns discovered, {} core collocates", ...)` — echoes outputs to mirror the entry log.
These logs follow the AI-hallmark entry/exit trace pattern: log all inputs on entry, log all outputs on exit, with no explanation of non-obvious decisions or unexpected conditions.
issues.suggestion: Remove the entry parameter echo logs and the result-count exit log. If observability is required, replace them with a single log at an appropriate level that captures a decision or condition worth noting (e.g., logging when the exploration finds zero collocates for a seed, which already exists at line 88). Parameter values are better inspected via a debugger; trace-level logging of inputs on every invocation creates noise in production logs.

issues.summary: Stale '(moved from BcqlPatternUtils in query package)' comment documents a completed migration as if it is still in progress
evidence: CqlUtils.java line 167: `// BCQL pattern utilities (moved from BcqlPatternUtils in query package)`
BcqlPatternUtils no longer exists anywhere in the repository. The move is complete.
The comment misleads readers into looking for a BcqlPatternUtils class that has been deleted.
issues.suggestion: Remove the comment. If the methods below it form a logical group, introduce a blank line or a non-historical comment (e.g. `// Pattern string manipulation`) without referencing the deleted source class.
